own_review_points: MemoryParquetFooterCache.put: the footerBytes.length() > maxSizeInBytes guard is good, but the human-ref point is to also short-circuit the lookup for oversized footers — v8 instead calls this guard 'redundant', the opposite conclusion
MemoryParquetFooterCache.put wraps ExecutionException in a raw RuntimeException, losing context; a footer-cache failure should not surface as an opaque RuntimeException to readers
ParquetFooterCache could be unified with the existing filesystem caching system rather than introducing a parallel cache (matches human ref)
Cache key = location + file size; relies on Iceberg data-file immutability — reasonable but must be documented since other parquet readers share MetadataReader
NoOpParquetFooterCache as enum is fine but the new readFooter overload's fallback-on-corrupt-bytes path needs a test (PR does add corrupt-cache fallback test)
notes: v8's lead point claims cache.get(key, footerBytes::copy) WILL replace an existing value — this is factually wrong: Guava Cache.get(K, Callable) is get-or-load and does NOT overwrite a present key, so the in-code comment is correct and v8's claim is a fabrication (-1 wrong). It also contradicts the human reference by calling the size guard 'redundant' when the human wants that very guard reused to skip the lookup (missed). Remaining points are mostly correct-but-trivial observations and 'consider' suggestions; one genuinely useful point (interface declares throws IOException that no impl throws) earns +1.
